title: Triarylmethanes Group  - Canada.ca
content:
## Triarylmethanes Group
- Triarylmethanes Group
These substances were identified for action under the Chemicals Management Plan (CMP). The screening assessment focused on 6 substances referred to collectively under the CMP as the Triarylmethanes Group.
- Summary of publications
- Information gathering Section 71 CMP 2023
- Guidelines Guidelines for the Reduction of Dyes Released from Pulp and Paper Mills
- Guidelines for the Reduction of Dyes Released from Pulp and Paper Mills

Return to footnote a referrer
Publication in the Canada Gazette , Part I: Vol. 158, No. 45 , of a notice of intent to amend the Domestic Substances List to apply the Significant New Activity provisions of CEPA to Malachite Green, Basic Violet 3, Basic Violet 4 and Basic Blue 7 and start of a 60-day public comment period.
Publication of the amended Guidelines for the Reduction of Dyes Released from Pulp and Paper Mills in the Canada Gazette , Part I: Vol. 158, No. 14. The amendments include Basic Blue 7, Basic Violet 3, Basic Violet 4, and Malachite Green.
Publication in Canada Gazette , Part I, of a notice under section 71 of CEPA.
Publication and start of 60-day public comment period on the proposed amended Guidelines for the Reduction of Dyes Released from Pulp and Paper Mills . The related notice was published in the Canada Gazette , Part I: Vol. 157, No. 18 . The proposed amendments include Basic Blue 7, Basic Violet 3, Basic Violet 4, and Malachite Green.
Publication in the Canada Gazette , Part I: Vol. 156, No. 14 of a proposed order adding Basic Violet 3, Malachite Green, Basic Violet 4, and Basic Blue 7 to Schedule 1 to CEPA and start of 60-day public comment.
Publication of the Final Screening Assessment for the Triarylmethanes Group . The Risk Management Approach for Certain Triarylmethanes, specifically CAS RNs 548-62-9, 569-64-2, 2390-59-2 and 2390-60-5 was also published for a 60-day public comment period. The related notice was published in the Canada Gazette , Part I: Vol. 154, No. 42 .
Publication and start of 60-day public comment period on the Draft Screening Assessment for the Triarylmethanes Group (including follow-up activities for CAS RNs 548-62-9, 2390-59-2, and 2390-60-5) and the Risk Management Scope for Certain Triarylmethanes, specifically: Basic Violet 3 (CAS RN 548-62-9), Malachite Green (CAS RN 569-64-2), Basic Violet 4 (CAS RN 2390-59-2), Basic Blue 7 (CAS RN 2390-60-5) . The related notice was published in the Canada Gazette , Part I: Vol. 152, No. 49 .

## Information gathering
In June 2023, a notice was published in the Canada Gazette , Part I: Vol. 157, No. 25 – June 24, 2023 under section 71 of CEPA. This notice applies to Basic Blue 7 (CAS RN 2390-60-5), Basic Violet 3 (CAS RN 548-62-9), Basic Violet 4 (CAS RN 2390-59-2) and Malachite Green (CAS RN 569-64-2). More information is available on the Information gathering with respect to certain substances under the Chemicals Management Plan web page.

### Guidelines for the Reduction of Dyes Released from Pulp and Paper Mills
In April 2024, the amended Guidelines for the Reduction of Dyes Released from Pulp and Paper Mills were published in the Canada Gazette , Part I. The Guidelines set out standards and good practices to observe to limit dye quantities released in the final effluent. The amendments include the addition of Basic Blue 7, Basic Violet 3, Basic Violet 4, and Malachite Green. The amended Guidelines supersede the original Guidelines for Reduction of Dyes Released from Pulp and Paper Mills 2013 , published in December 2013.
More information on the Guidelines, including amendments, is available on the Pulp and paper dyes: release guidelines overview web page.
A seventh substance, (CAS RN 632-99-5) was initially identified as being part of the Triarylmethanes Group at the outset of the third phase of the CMP . This substance was subsequently determined to be of low concern to both human health and the environment, through other approaches. The conclusion for this substance is provided in the Final Screening Assessment for the Rapid Screening of Substances with Limited General Population Exposure .
